reasoning: Bitcoin is currently trading between $80,000 and $95,000 as of mid-to-late December 2025. The all-time high hit in October 2025 was around $126,000. For Bitcoin to reach $150,000 by December 31, it would require a massive 60-70% surge in just the next 10 days, which is incredibly rare and has virtually no historical precedent under current market caps. Recent analyst reports (e.g., Citi on Dec 19, predicting $143k within next 12 months) project these price points years or months into the future, not days. The Polymarket price assigned an implied probability of just 1% over ten days ago, and since then, prices have remained subdued. Thus, the real probability is practically close to 0; I am submitting the minimum forecastable boundary of 0.02.
